title: Zephaniah 1
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Zephaniah
paragraphs: 1 This is the word of the LORD that came to Zephaniah son of Cushi, son of Gedaliah, son of Amariah, son of Hezekiah, in the days of Josiah son of Amon king of Judah:
2 "I will completely sweep away everything from the face of the earth," declares the LORD.

3 "I will sweep away man and beast. I will sweep away the birds of the air and the fish of the sea, and the idols with their wicked worshipers. I will cut off mankind from the face of the earth," declares the LORD.
4 "I will stretch out my hand against Judah and against all who live in Jerusalem. I will cut off from this place every remains of Baal, the names of the idol priests and pagan priests —
5 those who bow on the rooftops to worship the host of heaven, those who bow down and swear by the LORD but also swear by Milcom,
6 and those who turn back from following the LORD, neither seeking the LORD nor asking of him."
7 Be silent in the presence of the Lord GOD, for the Day of the LORD is near. The LORD has prepared a sacrifice. He has set apart his guests.
8 "On the Day of the LORD's sacrifice I will punish the princes, the sons of the king, and all who are dressed in foreign clothes.
9 On that day I will punish all who jump over the doorway, who fill the house of their master with violence and deceit.
10 On that day," declares the LORD, "a cry will go up from the Fish Gate, a wail from the Second District, and a loud crashing from the hills.
11 Wail, O people of the Hollow district. All your merchants will be silenced. All who weigh out silver will be cut off.
12 At that time I will search Jerusalem with lamps. I will punish the men settled in not caring. They say to themselves, 'The LORD will do nothing, either good or bad.'
13 Their wealth will be taken away and their houses laid waste. They will build houses but not live in them. They will plant vineyards but never drink their wine.
14 The great Day of the LORD is near — near and coming quickly. Listen, the Day of the LORD! Then the cry of the mighty will be bitter.
15 That day will be a day of wrath, a day of trouble and distress, a day of destruction and waste, a day of darkness and gloom, a day of clouds and blackness,
16 a day of horn blast and battle cry against the strong cities, and against the high corner towers.
17 I will bring such trouble on mankind that they will walk like the blind. They have sinned against the LORD. Their blood will be poured out like dust and their flesh like dung.

18 Neither their silver nor their gold will be able to save them on the Day of the LORD's wrath. The whole earth will be burned by the fire of his jealousy." For indeed, he will make a quick end of all who live on the earth.

title: Zephaniah 2
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Zephaniah
paragraphs: 1 Gather yourselves, gather together, O shameful nation,
2 before the order takes effect and the day passes like dry chaff. Before the burning anger of the LORD comes on you. Before the Day of the LORD's anger comes on you.
3 Seek the LORD, all you humble of the earth who do his justice. Seek righteousness. Seek humility. Perhaps you will be sheltered on the day of the LORD's anger.
4 For Gaza will be abandoned, and Ashkelon left in ruins. Ashdod will be driven out at noon. Ekron will be uprooted.
5 Woe to the people of the seacoast, O nation of the Cherethites! The word of the LORD is against you, O Canaan, land of the Philistines: "I will destroy you, and no one will be left."
6 So the seacoast will become a land of pastures, with wells for shepherds and folds for sheep.

7 The coast will belong to the remnant of the house of Judah. There they will find pasture. They will lie down in the evening among the houses of Ashkelon. The LORD their God will care for them and restore their captives.
8 "I have heard the shame of Moab and the insults of the Ammonites. They have mocked my people and threatened their borders.

9 Therefore, as surely as I live," declares the LORD of Hosts, the God of Israel, "surely Moab will be like Sodom and the Ammonites like Gomorrah — a place of weeds and salt pits, a wasteland forever. The remnant of my people will rob them. The rest of my nation will take over their land."
10 This they shall have in return for their pride, for mocking the people of the LORD of Hosts.
11 The LORD will be terrifying to them when he takes power from all the gods of the earth. Then the nations of every shore will bow in worship to him, each in its own place.
12 "You too, O Cushites, will be killed by my sword."
13 He will stretch out his hand against the north and destroy Assyria. He will make Nineveh an empty waste, as dry as a desert.

14 Herds will lie down in her midst, creatures of every kind. Both the desert owl and screech owl will roost on her pillars. Their calls will sound from the window. Empty waste will lie on the doorway. He will expose the beams of cedar.

15 This carefree city dwells securely. She thinks to herself, "I am it, and there is none besides me." What a ruin she has become, a resting place for animals. Everyone who passes by her hisses and shakes his fist.

title: Zephaniah 3
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Zephaniah
paragraphs: 1 Woe to the city of oppressors, rebellious and unclean!
2 She listened to no voice. She accepted no correction. She does not trust in the LORD. She has not drawn near to her God.
3 Her princes are roaring lions. Her judges are evening wolves, leaving nothing for the morning.
4 Her prophets are reckless, faithless men. Her priests make the sanctuary unholy. They do violence to the law.
5 The LORD within her is righteous. He does no wrong. He gives his justice morning by morning. He does not fail at dawn. Yet the unjust know no shame.
6 "I have cut off the nations. Their corner towers are destroyed. I have made their streets deserted with no one to pass through. Their cities are laid waste, with no man, no inhabitant.
7 I said, 'Surely you will fear me and accept correction.' Then her dwelling place would not be cut off, in spite of all I punished her for. But they rose early to corrupt all their deeds.

8 Therefore wait for me," declares the LORD, "until the day I rise to testify. For my decision is to gather nations, to assemble kingdoms, to pour out my wrath on them — all my burning anger. For all the earth will be burned by the fire of my jealousy.
9 For then I will restore pure lips to the peoples. They will all call on the name of the LORD and serve him shoulder to shoulder.
10 From beyond the rivers of Cush my worshipers, my scattered people, will bring me an offering.

11 On that day you will not be put to shame for any of the deeds by which you have sinned against me. For then I will remove from you those who rejoice in their pride. You will never again be proud on my holy mountain.
12 But I will leave within you a gentle and humble people. They will trust in the name of the LORD.
13 The remnant of Israel will no longer do wrong or speak lies. No deceitful tongue will be found in their mouths. But they will feed and lie down, with no one to make them tremble."
14 Sing for joy, O Daughter of Zion. Shout aloud, O Israel! Be glad and rejoice with all your heart, O Daughter of Jerusalem!
15 The LORD has taken away your punishment. He has turned back your enemy. Israel's King, the LORD, is among you. No longer will you fear any harm.
16 On that day they will say to Jerusalem: "Do not fear, O Zion. Do not let your hands fall limp.
17 The LORD your God is among you. He is mighty to save. He will rejoice over you with gladness. He will quiet you with his love. He will rejoice over you with singing."
18 "I will gather those among you who grieve over the appointed feasts, so you will no longer suffer shame.
19 Look, at that time I will deal with all who hurt you. I will save the lame and gather the scattered. I will appoint praise and fame for the disgraced throughout the earth.

20 At that time I will bring you in. Yes, at that time I will gather you. For I will give you fame and praise among all the peoples of the earth, when I restore your captives before your very eyes," says the LORD.
